questions.stem_en: What is illegal regarding earphones or earplugs while driving?
questions.stem_zhHant: 駕駛時佩戴耳機或耳塞有何違法規定？

options.text_en: Wearing an earplug in one ear only
options.text_zhHant: 僅在一隻耳朵佩戴耳塞

options.text_en: Wearing earplugs or earphones in both ears
options.text_zhHant: 在雙耳佩戴耳塞或耳機

options.text_en: Wearing any type of headset at any time
options.text_zhHant: 在任何時候佩戴任何類型的耳機

options.text_en: Using a hands-free Bluetooth device

questions.explanation_en: It is illegal to wear earplugs in both ears or earphones covering both ears while driving. You may use a single earpiece for hands-free phone use.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 駕駛時在雙耳佩戴耳塞或覆蓋雙耳的耳機是違法的。您可以使用單耳耳機進行免持通話。
questions.explanation_zh: 驾驶时在双耳佩戴耳塞或覆盖双耳的耳机是违法的。您可以使用单耳耳机进行免持通话。

questions.stem_en: What should you do if you feel drowsy while driving?
questions.stem_zhHant: 駕駛時如果感到昏昏欲睡，您應該怎麼做？

options.text_en: Turn up the radio and keep driving
options.text_zhHant: 調高收音機音量繼續駕駛

options.text_en: Open the window for fresh air and continue
options.text_zhHant: 打開車窗呼吸新鮮空氣後繼續駕駛

options.text_en: Pull over to a safe area and rest
options.text_zhHant: 靠邊停在安全區域並休息

options.text_en: Drive faster to reach your destination sooner
options.text_zhHant: 加速以便更快到達目的地

questions.explanation_en: If you feel drowsy while driving, the safest action is to pull over to a safe area and rest. Tricks like turning up the radio or opening the window are not effective substitutes for rest.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 如果駕駛時感到昏昏欲睡，最安全的做法是靠邊停在安全區域並休息。調高收音機音量或打開車窗等技巧無法有效替代休息。
questions.explanation_zh: 如果驾驶时感到昏昏欲睡，最安全的做法是靠边停在安全区域并休息。调高收音机音量或打开车窗等技巧无法有效替代休息。

questions.stem_en: Who is required to report medical conditions that may affect driving ability to the DMV?
questions.stem_zhHant: 誰需要向車輛管理局報告可能影響駕駛能力的健康狀況？

options.text_en: Only the driver themselves
Physicians for patients aged 14 and older
options.text_zhHant: 醫師須報告14歲及以上患者的情況

options.text_en: Family members of the driver
Law enforcement officers only

questions.explanation_en: Physicians are required by law to report patients aged 14 and older who have medical conditions that may affect their ability to drive safely.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 法律規定醫師必須報告14歲及以上、患有可能影響安全駕駛能力之健康狀況的患者。
questions.explanation_zh: 法律规定医师必须报告14岁及以上、患有可能影响安全驾驶能力之健康状况的患者。

questions.stem_en: Where should your hands be positioned for hand-to-hand (push/pull) steering?
questions.stem_zhHant: 使用推拉式轉向時，雙手應放在方向盤的什麼位置？

options.text_en: 10 and 2 o'clock
12 o'clock with both hands together
9 and 3 o'clock or 8 and 4 o'clock
One hand at 12 o'clock only

questions.explanation_en: For hand-to-hand (push/pull) steering, your hands should be positioned at 9 and 3 o'clock or 8 and 4 o'clock. This provides optimal control and keeps hands away from the airbag deployment zone.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 使用推拉式轉向時，雙手應放在9點和3點位置或8點和4點位置。這樣可提供最佳控制力，並使雙手遠離安全氣囊展開區域。
questions.explanation_zh: 使用推拉式转向时，双手应放在9点和3点位置或8点和4点位置。这样可提供最佳控制力，并使双手远离安全气囊展开区域。

questions.stem_en: When should you use hand-over-hand steering?
questions.stem_zhHant: 什麼時候應該使用交叉手轉向？

options.text_en: When driving on the freeway at high speeds
options.text_zhHant: 在高速公路上高速行駛時

options.text_en: When turning at low speeds, parking, or recovering from a skid
options.text_zhHant: 在低速轉彎、停車或從打滑中恢復時

options.text_en: When backing up in a straight line
Only when making U-turns

questions.explanation_en: Hand-over-hand steering is appropriate when turning at low speeds, parking, or recovering from a skid. It allows for greater steering wheel rotation.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 交叉手轉向適用於低速轉彎、停車或從打滑中恢復。這種方式可實現更大幅度的方向盤旋轉。
questions.explanation_zh: 交叉手转向适用于低速转弯、停车或从打滑中恢复。这种方式可实现更大幅度的方向盘旋转。

questions.stem_en: When is one-hand steering acceptable?
questions.stem_zhHant: 什麼時候可以使用單手轉向？

options.text_en: When eating or drinking while driving
options.text_zhHant: 駕駛時吃東西或喝水時

options.text_en: When talking on a cell phone
When backing up and turning to look behind you
Whenever you feel comfortable doing so
options.text_zhHant: 任何時候只要您覺得方便

questions.explanation_en: One-hand steering is only acceptable when you need to turn your body to look behind you while backing up. At all other times, both hands should be on the steering wheel.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 僅在倒車時需要轉身向後看的情況下才可以使用單手轉向。其他任何時候，雙手都應放在方向盤上。
questions.explanation_zh: 仅在倒车时需要转身向后看的情况下才可以使用单手转向。其他任何时候，双手都应放在方向盘上。

questions.stem_en: What should you NEVER do with the steering wheel?
questions.stem_zhHant: 對方向盤絕對不能做什麼？

options.text_en: Use hand-to-hand steering on curves
options.text_zhHant: 在彎道上使用推拉式轉向

options.text_en: Let it spin freely or remove both hands from it
options.text_zhHant: 讓方向盤自由旋轉或雙手同時離開方向盤

options.text_en: Hold it with both hands at 9 and 3
options.text_zhHant: 雙手放在9點和3點位置握住方向盤

options.text_en: Use hand-over-hand steering when parking
options.text_zhHant: 停車時使用交叉手轉向

questions.explanation_en: You should never let the steering wheel spin freely to straighten out or remove both hands from the steering wheel while driving. Always maintain control of the wheel.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 絕不能讓方向盤自由旋轉來回正或在駕駛時雙手同時離開方向盤。始終要保持對方向盤的控制。
questions.explanation_zh: 绝不能让方向盘自由旋转来回正或在驾驶时双手同时离开方向盘。始终要保持对方向盘的控制。

questions.stem_en: How far before a turn should you signal in city driving?
questions.stem_zhHant: 在城市道路駕駛中，轉彎前多遠應該打方向燈？

options.text_en: At least 50 feet
At least 100 feet
At least 200 feet
At least 300 feet

questions.explanation_en: In city driving, you must signal at least 100 feet before making a turn so that other drivers have time to react to your intentions.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在城市道路駕駛中，轉彎前至少100英尺就應該打方向燈，以便其他駕駛人有時間對您的意圖做出反應。
questions.explanation_zh: 在城市道路驾驶中，转弯前至少100英尺就应该打方向灯，以便其他驾驶人有时间对您的意图做出反应。

questions.stem_en: How many seconds before changing lanes on the freeway should you signal?
questions.stem_zhHant: 在高速公路上變換車道前應提前幾秒打方向燈？

options.text_en: At least 2 seconds
At least 3 seconds
At least 5 seconds
At least 10 seconds

questions.explanation_en: On the freeway, you should signal at least 5 seconds before changing lanes. This gives other drivers enough time to adjust to your intended lane change at higher speeds.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在高速公路上，變換車道前至少應提前5秒打方向燈。這讓其他駕駛人在高速行駛時有足夠時間對您的變道意圖做出調整。
questions.explanation_zh: 在高速公路上，变换车道前至少应提前5秒打方向灯。这让其他驾驶人在高速行驶时有足够时间对您的变道意图做出调整。

questions.stem_en: The hand signal for a right turn is:
questions.stem_zhHant: 右轉的手勢信號是：

options.text_en: Left arm extended straight out the window
Left arm bent downward at 90 degrees
Left arm bent upward at 90 degrees
Right arm extended straight out the window

questions.explanation_en: The hand signal for a right turn is your left arm bent upward at a 90-degree angle out the driver's window.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 右轉的手勢信號是左臂向上彎曲成90度角伸出駕駛座車窗外。
questions.explanation_zh: 右转的手势信号是左臂向上弯曲成90度角伸出驾驶座车窗外。

questions.stem_en: When should you turn off your turn signal?
questions.stem_zhHant: 什麼時候應該關閉方向燈？

options.text_en: Right before you start your turn
After completing the turn or lane change
options.text_zhHant: 完成轉彎或變換車道之後

options.text_en: It will turn off automatically, no action needed
options.text_zhHant: 方向燈會自動關閉，不需要操作

options.text_en: While you are in the middle of the turn

questions.explanation_en: You should turn off your signal after completing the turn or lane change. Leaving it on can confuse other drivers about your intentions.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 完成轉彎或變換車道後應關閉方向燈。持續亮著方向燈可能會讓其他駕駛人對您的意圖產生困惑。
questions.explanation_zh: 完成转弯或变换车道后应关闭方向灯。持续亮着方向灯可能会让其他驾驶人对您的意图产生困惑。

questions.stem_en: A driver extends their left arm straight out from the window. This hand signal means:
questions.stem_zhHant: 駕駛員將左臂從車窗伸直。這個手勢信號表示：

options.text_en: Slowing down or stopping
Left turn
Right turn
Lane change

questions.explanation_en: Left arm extended straight out indicates a left turn.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 左臂直直伸出表示左轉。
questions.explanation_zh: 左臂直直伸出表示左转。

questions.stem_en: A driver extends their left arm out the window with the arm bent upward at a 90-degree angle. This signal indicates:
questions.stem_zhHant: 駕駛員將左臂從車窗伸出並向上彎曲成90度角。這個信號表示：

options.text_en: Left turn
Slowing or stopping
Right turn
Hazard ahead

questions.explanation_en: Left arm bent upward at 90 degrees signals a right turn.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 左臂向上彎曲90度表示右轉。
questions.explanation_zh: 左臂向上弯曲90度表示右转。

questions.stem_en: A driver extends their left arm out the window with the arm bent downward. This hand signal indicates:
questions.stem_zhHant: 駕駛員將左臂從車窗伸出並向下彎曲。這個手勢信號表示：

options.text_en: Left turn
Right turn
Slowing down or stopping
Backing up

questions.explanation_en: Left arm bent downward signals slowing down or stopping.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 左臂向下彎曲表示減速或停車。
questions.explanation_zh: 左臂向下弯曲表示减速或停车。

questions.stem_en: When must you turn on your headlights?
questions.stem_zhHant: 什麼時候必須開啟車頭燈？

options.text_en: Only when it is completely dark outside
30 minutes after sunset, 30 minutes before sunrise, or when you cannot see 1,000 feet ahead
options.text_zhHant: 日落後30分鐘、日出前30分鐘，或當您無法看到前方1,000英尺時

options.text_en: Only during heavy rain or snow
Whenever other drivers have their headlights on
options.text_zhHant: 當其他駕駛人開啟車頭燈時

questions.explanation_en: You must turn on your headlights 30 minutes after sunset, 30 minutes before sunrise, or any time conditions prevent you from seeing 1,000 feet ahead.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 您必須在日落後30分鐘、日出前30分鐘或任何無法看到前方1,000英尺的情況下開啟車頭燈。
questions.explanation_zh: 您必须在日落后30分钟、日出前30分钟或任何无法看到前方1,000英尺的情况下开启车头灯。

questions.stem_en: You must dim your high-beam headlights within how many feet of an oncoming vehicle?
questions.stem_zhHant: 距迎面車輛多少英尺內必須將遠光燈切換為近光燈？

options.text_en: 200 feet
300 feet
500 feet
1,000 feet

questions.explanation_en: You must dim your high-beam headlights within 500 feet of an oncoming vehicle to avoid blinding the other driver.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 距迎面車輛500英尺內必須將遠光燈切換為近光燈，以避免使對方駕駛人眩目。
questions.explanation_zh: 距迎面车辆500英尺内必须将远光灯切换为近光灯，以避免使对方驾驶人眩目。

questions.stem_en: When must you use low-beam headlights?
questions.stem_zhHant: 什麼時候必須使用近光燈？

options.text_en: Only on rural roads at night
Within 300 feet of a vehicle you are following, or in fog, rain, or snow when windshield wipers are on
options.text_zhHant: 距您前方車輛300英尺以內，或在霧、雨或雪中雨刷啟用時

options.text_en: Only during the daytime
Only when driving on the freeway
options.text_zhHant: 僅在高速公路上駕駛時

questions.explanation_en: You must use low-beam headlights within 300 feet of a vehicle you are following, and in fog, rain, or snow when your windshield wipers are in continuous use.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 距您前方車輛300英尺以內時必須使用近光燈，以及在霧、雨或雪中持續使用雨刷時也必須使用近光燈。
questions.explanation_zh: 距您前方车辆300英尺以内时必须使用近光灯，以及在雾、雨或雪中持续使用雨刷时也必须使用近光灯。

questions.stem_en: Is it legal to drive using only parking lights in California?
questions.stem_zhHant: 在加州僅開啟駐車燈行駛是否合法？

options.text_en: Yes, parking lights are sufficient at night
options.text_zhHant: 是的，駐車燈在夜間足夠了

options.text_en: Yes, but only in well-lit urban areas
options.text_zhHant: 是的，但僅限於照明良好的城市地區

options.text_en: No, it is illegal to drive using only parking lights
options.text_zhHant: 不，僅開啟駐車燈行駛是違法的

options.text_en: Yes, but only if you are driving under 25 mph
options.text_zhHant: 是的，但僅限於時速25英里以下

questions.explanation_en: It is illegal to drive using only your parking lights in California. Parking lights are designed for parked vehicles only; headlights must be used when driving.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在加州僅開啟駐車燈行駛是違法的。駐車燈僅設計用於停放的車輛；駕駛時必須使用車頭燈。
questions.explanation_zh: 在加州仅开启驻车灯行驶是违法的。驻车灯仅设计用于停放的车辆；驾驶时必须使用车头灯。

questions.stem_en: When should you use your horn?
questions.stem_zhHant: 什麼時候應該使用喇叭？

options.text_en: To greet friends or express frustration
options.text_zhHant: 向朋友打招呼或表達不滿

options.text_en: To avoid collisions or alert others on narrow mountain roads
options.text_zhHant: 為了避免碰撞或在狹窄的山路上提醒其他人

options.text_en: To signal that you are passing another vehicle
options.text_zhHant: 示意您正在超越另一輛車

options.text_en: To tell a slow driver to move out of the way

questions.explanation_en: Use your horn only when necessary to avoid collisions or to alert other drivers on narrow mountain roads where you cannot see at least 200 feet ahead.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 僅在必要時使用喇叭以避免碰撞，或在無法看到前方至少200英尺的狹窄山路上提醒其他駕駛人。
questions.explanation_zh: 仅在必要时使用喇叭以避免碰撞，或在无法看到前方至少200英尺的狭窄山路上提醒其他驾驶人。

questions.stem_en: What should you do if you see a hazard ahead on the road?
questions.stem_zhHant: 如果您看到前方道路上有危險，應該怎麼做？

options.text_en: Honk your horn continuously
Flash your headlights at other drivers
options.text_zhHant: 向其他駕駛人閃車頭燈

options.text_en: Turn on emergency flashers and tap your brakes 3 to 4 times
options.text_zhHant: 開啟緊急閃光燈並輕踩煞車3至4次

options.text_en: Quickly change lanes without signaling
options.text_zhHant: 不打信號快速變換車道

questions.explanation_en: If you see a hazard ahead, turn on your emergency flashers and tap your brakes 3 to 4 times to alert drivers behind you that they need to slow down.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 如果您看到前方有危險，請開啟緊急閃光燈並輕踩煞車3至4次，提醒後方駕駛人需要減速。
questions.explanation_zh: 如果您看到前方有危险，请开启紧急闪光灯并轻踩刹车3至4次，提醒后方驾驶人需要减速。

questions.stem_en: If your vehicle breaks down on the road, what should you do first?
questions.stem_zhHant: 如果您的車輛在路上拋錨，首先應該怎麼做？

options.text_en: Get out of the vehicle immediately and walk to safety
options.text_zhHant: 立即下車走到安全的地方

options.text_en: Turn on your emergency flashers and safely pull off the road
options.text_zhHant: 開啟緊急閃光燈並安全地駛離道路

options.text_en: Stay in the lane and call for help
options.text_zhHant: 留在車道上並打電話求助

options.text_en: Wave to other drivers for assistance
options.text_zhHant: 向其他駕駛人揮手求助

questions.explanation_en: If your vehicle breaks down, turn on your emergency flashers to alert other drivers and safely pull off the road as far as possible from traffic.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 如果您的車輛拋錨，請開啟緊急閃光燈提醒其他駕駛人，並安全地盡可能遠離車流駛離道路。
questions.explanation_zh: 如果您的车辆抛锚，请开启紧急闪光灯提醒其他驾驶人，并安全地尽可能远离车流驶离道路。
